description: Repo-level commercial source of truth for the first Sense360 shop launch. Pins the customer-facing launch SKU, shop title, shop posture, candidate-bundle visibility, customer WebFlash URL, and the approved / forbidden ecommerce claims so product naming, shop copy, and WebFlash links stay consistent before customer-facing pages are published. This file is documentation / commercial-policy only: it adds no product YAMLs, firmware builds, artifacts, releases, WebFlash rows, or stable promotions, and it flips no hardware schematic_status or product-catalog lifecycle. See docs/shop-commercial-source-of-truth.md.

launch_product.notes: Canonical launch shop product is the complete Bathroom PoE room bundle only. The firmware_config and artifact_name are the existing Release-One stable WebFlash build (config/webflash-builds.json) and the production catalog entry (config/product-catalog.json). S360-410 (PoE PSU) remains cataloged_unverified in config/hardware-catalog.json, so commercial copy must not overclaim hardware certification, compliance, or broad verification.

S360-KIT-BATH-POE.notes: Kit-intent spelling used in config/kit-intent-matrix.json (kit_id) and mirrored by the WebFlash kit presets. Treated as a legacy/alias spelling of the canonical room-bundle SKU; resolve any external reference to S360-KIT-BATH-P. Not renamed by this file.

S360-KIT-CEILING-VENTIQ-ROOMIQ-POE.notes: Appears as a WebFlash kits.json kit id; it mirrors the firmware config string (Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-RoomIQ) too closely to use as a customer-facing shop SKU. Do not use it as the shop SKU; the customer-facing SKU is S360-KIT-BATH-P.

webflash.reserved_portal_purpose: Reserved for a future customer portal / account / setup / support experience. It is NOT the firmware flashing URL unless a future decision changes this.
webflash.notes: Customer-facing browser flashing points at https://flash.sense360.com. The GitHub Pages origin https://sense360store.github.io/WebFlash/ is the deployment origin / technical fallback only. WebFlash repo, manifest.json, firmware/sources.json, and config/webflash-builds.json are unchanged by this file.

candidate_bundle_visibility.notes: Candidate room bundles are planning / preview / stable-candidate records in config/room-bundle-skus.json, not public sale commitments. Hidden from normal shop navigation by default. Optional waitlist / coming-soon pages may exist only if explicitly labelled as above; never with a buy button.

allowed: Tracks humidity, temperature, presence, and bathroom air-quality signals
Designed for Home Assistant / ESPHome users
Supports browser-based firmware installation with WebFlash
Can help inform automations such as ventilation reminders or alerts
Includes Sense360 Core, RoomIQ, VentIQ, and PoE PSU
claims.not_allowed_principle: No claim may imply a certified life-safety, building-code, medical, or compliance function. Forbidden claims require separate legal/commercial approval and evidence before use.
claims.notes: "Controls extractor fans", "Mains fan control", and "TRIAC fan control" are forbidden for the base Bathroom PoE kit; the base kit ships no fan driver. Mold / condensation claims and any certified-function claim are forbidden without separate legal/commercial approval and evidence.

fan_control_copy.notes: Fan-control products may be mentioned only as future / preview / installer / manual-candidate where already recorded (config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json, docs/manual-install-fan-candidates.md, config/room-bundle-fan-variants.json). Fan control is NOT part of the base Bathroom PoE kit. TRIAC (S360-320) is not added as recommended, default, stable, or customer-facing; it remains blocked by HW-005.

guardrails.notes: Hard guardrails this file must never break. The canonical room-bundle SKU stays S360-KIT-BATH-P; S360-410 is not claimed schematic-verified; no hardware/compliance certification, mold prevention/detection, or condensation prevention/elimination is claimed; fan control is not claimed for the base kit; candidate bundles are not exposed as buyable; individual boards are not sold as primary launch products; TRIAC is not added to any customer-facing recommendation; no firmware is published; the WebFlash repo is untouched.
